note: Per-card-type warm-throughput baselines for the perf-regression gate (scripts/perf_regression.py). The file nests one models block per card type under a 'cards' key (p150a, p300c, ...); the gate detects the card it is running on at runtime (tt-smi / kernel sysfs, mirroring tt_bio/main.py::_detect_p300_devices) and compares only against the matching card's baseline. A P300c baseline must never be judged against a P150a run — the P150 is a smaller chip and would read as a false 20-34% regression that is just the card, not the code. If a detected card type has no recorded baseline yet the gate FAILS loudly (every model NO BASELINE) rather than silently skipping. Protocol: scripts/perf_regression.py --model <m>; fold models fold examples/trpcage.yaml (20 aa, single-sequence) at 1 recycling cycle / 10 sampling steps / 1 diffusion sample; esmc-600m embeds 8x ubiquitin (76 aa) at batch 8. Each model: load once in-process, 2 warmup folds (absorb first-kernel compile, excluded), 5 timed folds, report median throughput. Model load + first-compile are excluded — this is a warm dispatch/throughput number, not a cold-start or production fold. It catches kernel/dispatch regressions; accuracy is scripts/release_gate.py's job. Update a baseline only via --update-baseline --note "<why this perf change is intended>" on a card of that type; never silently. Seed a new card type by running the gate on that card with --update-baseline.

p300c.note: Seeded from warm runs on main HEAD (release 0.2.5) on a Blackhole P300c card (qb2).
